reasoning: As of October 2025, the Wikipedia article 'List of infectious diseases' lists no vaccine for Human parainfluenza virus infection Croup. A review of recent scientific literature and the CDC (as of 2024/2025) explicitly states that there is no licensed vaccine for HPIV, and candidates are currently only in pre-clinical or early clinical development. Given the extensive timeline required for late-stage vaccine trials and authorization (spanning years), it is extremely implausible that a viable vaccine will be developed, approved, and added to Wikipedia between November 2025 and February 2026. The only significant pathway to a 'Yes' resolution is an uncorrected error or vandalism on the Wikipedia page on the specific resolution dates. Consequently, the probability is set near the minimum allowed value (0.05), increasing marginally for more distant dates to account for the slight structural uncertainty and longer horizons.
